column30: First full submission. Beats baseline on Task1 (6.204) and Task3 (6.167). Below baseline on Task2 (6.210) -- FFA branches randomly initialized, undertrained at 60 epochs. Topology (COR/INF) is the clear weak point across both tasks -- no topology-aware loss used yet (clDice not added). Task3 used heuristic OD detector, not a trained model.

submission: (backfilled from leaderboard row, exact local zip filename not recorded at submission time)
notes: Second submission, backfilled 2026-07-18 from the live leaderboard row (was never logged when submitted). Task1/Task2 both improved a lot over submission 1 (clDice topology loss + vein-weighted pos_weight/clDice ratio, per commits 000f2b2/020bbda) -- Task2 in particular went from below-baseline (5.678) to above (6.982). But Task3 REGRESSED (6.580 -> 6.076) despite the OD detector being unchanged between the two submissions -- root cause traced to: (1) the Kaggle kernel that trained this run shows status CANCEL_ACKNOWLEDGED, and its log confirms only fold0 was ever trained for both Task1 and Task2 (the 'Task2 5-fold ensemble' code in commit 18190be was added but never actually executed across folds 1-4) -- so this submission's masks came from single-fold models, not an ensemble, which may have been noisier/worse-thresholded than submission 1's masks despite better pixel/topology metrics. (2) run_task3.py still called the unused heuristic OD detector at submission time (fixed same day, see next entry).

Third submission, scored 2026-07-19 02:18 (took ~3h to evaluate, unclear why -- not resubmitted since no error state was shown, seems to have just been a slow queue). Task3 improved hugely as predicted (6.076 -> 6.537, +0.46) confirming the SegFormer OD fix was real. BUT Task1 (7.093->6.744, -0.25) and Task2 (6.982->6.840, -0.14) both REGRESSED, netting the round score to essentially flat (6.664->6.700, +0.036 -- noise-level). Root cause: the recovered fold0 best.pth checkpoints were NOT equivalent to what produced submission 2 (confirmed earlier via ~15/255 mean pixel diff vs submission_compressed.zip) -- per-channel breakdown shows this wasn't a uniform regression but a channel tradeoff: Task1 V_COR cratered (0.538->0.322) and V_INF got much worse (0.453->0.676) while A_COR/A_INF both improved and AV_DSC improved (0.688->0.729); Task2 A_COR cratered (0.614->0.431) and A_INF got much worse (0.379->0.566) while V_COR/V_INF both improved slightly and AV_DSC improved (0.705->0.740). Likely cause for Task2: the vein_pos_weight=8.0/vein_topology_ratio=2.0 asymmetric loss (commit 020bbda) overcorrected -- pushed vein topology up at real cost to artery topology, net negative given the score formula. Task1 has no such asymmetric config, so its matching A-up/V-down pattern is likely just an artifact of the aborted epoch 61-65 continuation (commit 983891c) pushing the model past whatever point best.pth was previously checkpointed at, not a deliberate tradeoff. NEXT STEP: consider dialing back the vein/artery asymmetry for Task2, and be more careful about which exact checkpoint state gets used for a submission -- 'best.pth' by val_loss does not track topology metrics reliably.

Fourth submission, scored 2026-07-20 06:07. Task1 (6.744->7.016, +0.27) and Task2 (6.840->6.934, +0.09) BOTH improved as intended -- confirms the vein-rebalance fix (mild fine-tune for Task1, fresh retrain with milder asymmetry for Task2) worked directionally, validated locally before submitting and now confirmed on the real leaderboard too. BUT Task3 REGRESSED this time (6.537->6.355, -0.18) even though the OD detector was UNCHANGED from submission 3 -- the only Task3 input that changed was the new Task2 masks. Per-biomarker breakdown: vein_density improved, but AVR/artery_fractal_dimension/artery_density all got notably worse (artery_density_MAE alone jumped 0.49->0.62). Likely explanation: the retrained Task2 model, despite better pixel/topology segmentation metrics, produces an artery mask with subtly different width/coverage characteristics at the threshold=127 binarization -- CRAE/AVR are very sensitive to exact vessel-caliber measurement (top-6 widest arteriolar segments in Zone C), so a small shift there can hurt biomarker accuracy without showing up much in DSC/Sen/Spec. Net effect: round score barely moved again (6.700->6.719, +0.019 -- three submissions in a row now essentially flat around 6.7 despite real, validated component-level improvements each time -- gains in one task keep getting offset by losses in another). NEXT STEP: test whether feeding Task3 from Task1's masks (or an ensemble of both) gives better biomarker accuracy than Task2's, using local held-out GT biomarker labels -- don't just assume Task2-sourced Task3 is always better.

Fifth submission, packaged locally, not yet uploaded/scored. Full 5-fold ensemble (folds 0-4, both tasks, all individually validated on their own held-out cases before inclusion) + horizontal-flip TTA, replacing the single-fold0 masks used in submissions 2-4. Trained folds 1-3 locally, folds 2+4 in parallel on Kaggle (via new minimal kernel aaronajit/gave2-fold-train, one fold-pair per run -- fits in a single ~5.7h session, no session-splitting needed). EARLY VALIDATION GATE (user's idea) run after fold0+1+2 were ready: evaluated fold0-alone vs fold0+1+2-ensembled on fold3's held-out cases (genuinely unseen by all three, unlike testing on fold0's own held-out set which folds1/2 were trained on) -- vein topology improved dramatically in both tasks (Task1 vein COR +0.112/INF -0.106, Task2 vein COR +0.104/INF -0.106), confirming the ensembling hypothesis before committing to folds 3-4's ~20h of additional compute. Real operational hiccups this round: (1) first Kaggle fold2 attempt failed in ~1min because local code changes (vein-weighting flags, OD fix) were never pushed to the GitHub repo Kaggle clones from -- fixed by committing+pushing before every Kaggle kernel push going forward; (2) local Task2 fold1 training hit the same VRAM-ceiling slowdown as before (2.3-4x epoch-time creep at patch_size=384) -- now applied --patch-size 320 proactively from the start for all new Task2 folds rather than discovering it again; (3) GitHub repo renamed aaronjji/gave2-challenge -> aaronjji/rk8391-devbox and READMEs scrubbed of MICCAI/GAVE2 references (user's request, reduce discoverability during active competition) -- all Kaggle notebook clone URLs updated to match. Checkpoint choice per fold: used final.pth except Task2 fold3 (best.pth/epoch55 -- final/epoch60 had a late-training val_loss spike to 2.145, clearly worse). UPDATE THIS ROW once the real score lands.
